markets uh we have stocks up this
morning commodities mixed crypto mix
dollar down global bond yields up uh big
news this morning we have ukraine and
russia striving for ceasefire talks uh
that began today in turkey those talks
have a minimum go of improving uh the
improvement on the humanitarian front as
you recall russia's bomb some civilian
targets and have really threatened some
of the evacuation routes out of some of
the more beleaguered cities so hopefully
we get some improvement there uh more
improvement and obviously markets are
responding very positively to that um
you know we continue to have signs of
strategic splits emerging within nato's
ranks um you know france germany italy
all incrementally open to dialogue this
morning well obviously president biden's
most recent remarks uk poland most
central and eastern european nations
continue to be extremely skeptical uh
skeptical of putin's intentions there so
as long as we continue to have those
divisions emerging within nato it's very
unlikely that things get worse uh from a
geopolitical perspective in terms of the
west response in terms of sanctions and
things of that nature so that again on
the margin is positive for the markets
with respect to big movers today you
have germany's tax index up a full two
percent uh the 10-year boone yield up 11
basis points to six zero spot six eight
percent that's been driven by uh the
repricing of european policy rate
expectations uh we have one year one
month uh overnight index swap forwards
up 11 basis points to zero spot 85
percent there um in terms of euro euro
swaps
on the macro front with the latest
consumer confidence data out of europe
we're marginally confirming of the
pending slowdown in consumer spending
across the pond as previously discussed
the inflation shock to europe has been
more significant than any other region
in the world so we got germany's gfk
consumer confidence index came out
that's -15.5 in the month of march here
that's the lowest point we've seen since
june of 20. uh france's uh consumer
confidence number came uh ticked down to
91. uh that's the lowest point we've
seen since november 20 as well so
taking consumer confidence ratings uh
down to multi-year lows here in europe
uh the inflation dynamics there taking
it to the chart uh what i'm showing in
this chart in the upper panel here is
the city inflation surprise index for
the u.s that's the blue line the uh the
european uh city inflation surprise
index for the eurozone that's the red
line there as you can see the u.s has
been kind of huddling around its
all-time high really since going back to
july of last year but as you can see in
in surprise terms the inflation surprise
in europe has been significantly more
pronounced than what we've uh what we've
seen uh in the u.s economy and as a
function of that we've seen a more
statistically significant uh rise in
bond boon yields relative to treasury
yields we got a 4.7 sigma move on a
trailing three-year basis uh in 10-year
german boon yields well to the two-sigma
move we've got into your treasury yields
and so this is something that uh just in
terms of the bond market spots in europe
the surprise and momentum and inflation
pressure in europe has obviously been
something that's uh catalyzed a lot of
pain across fixed income markets across
the world we would expect that to wane
as we progress throughout the year and
inflation comes down but that's very
that's something that we expect to wane
in a material basis over the near term
so this is going to continue to be a
problem uh with respect to today's
probable range setups uh still bearish
fam's s p 500 but it still looks like

now we'll see um if we get that change
